target_game.name: Crazy Kick! Fun Football game
target_game.core_loop: Swipe to control the ball, dodge defenders, and score a goal to advance to the next level.
target_game.audience_proxy: Casual mobile gamers, skewing male, ages 8-30, looking for quick, physics-based sports action.
target_game.visual_style: 3D hyper-casual, low-poly, bright and colorful with simple stickman characters.

palette.description: Vibrant and sunny palette dominated by sandy yellow and sky blue, with bright green UI accents.

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · in-game

centroid_hook.summary: Rapid-fire block placement clearing a complex green grid.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Green blocks are slotted into a grid at high speed, filling gaps and clearing lines.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 3d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Rapid-fire block placement clearing a complex green grid."

top_archetypes.label: Fail · 3D-render

centroid_hook.summary: A cat jumps on a desk, startling a man who falls backward.
centroid_hook.visual_action: An orange cat leaps onto a desk, followed by a close-up of its face and then the butler falling out of his chair.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 381d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A cat jumps on a desk, startling a man who falls backward."

top_archetypes.label: Curiosity · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Reveals a hidden underground bunker beneath the Eiffel Tower.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A vertical cross-section view pans down from the Eiffel Tower to show a multi-level subterranean base.

top_archetypes.rationale: 2 creatives across 2 unique advertisers, average age 286d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Reveals a hidden underground bunker beneath the Eiffel Tower."

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · mixed

centroid_hook.summary: A hand uses a stylus to move colorful buses in a crowded parking lot, accompanied by a question about relaxation.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A hand with a stylus interacts with a grid of buses, sliding them to clear paths and send them to a queue.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: Hey! Played a game that Actually makes you Feel relaxed?
centroid_hook.voiceover_transcript: Hey! Have you ever played a game that actually makes you feel relaxed?

top_archetypes.rationale: 3 creatives across 3 unique advertisers, average age 81d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A hand uses a stylus to move colorful buses in a crowded parking lot, accompanie"

top_archetypes.label: Fail · in-game

centroid_hook.summary: Player attempts to move buses in a crowded lot but immediately causes a collision.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A hand cursor selects a purple bus which moves out, then selects a red bus that crashes into another.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: WHY THIS LEVEL IS SO HARD, CAN YOU WIN?

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 259d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Player attempts to move buses in a crowded lot but immediately causes a collisio"

game_fit_scores.rationale: This is a block/grid puzzle satisfaction hook — its mechanics (slotting blocks, clearing lines) have zero overlap with Crazy Kick's swipe-to-shoot, physics-based soccer loop, so a faithful adaptation isn't viable. The green/yellow/indigo palette partially rhymes with the game's sunny yellow + green accents, but the calm puzzle aesthetic clashes with the energetic stickman sports vibe and male 8–30 sports audience. Pass on adapting this archetype directly; only the "genius/psychopath" text-overlay meme could be borrowed onto a trick-shot montage if you want to test the satisfaction angle.

This is a Playrix-style domestic slapstick fail hook (cat startles butler) built for casual puzzle/decor audiences skewing female 30+ — almost zero overlap with Crazy Kick's male 8-30 sports-arcade audience. The 3D-render interior palette and character archetypes clash with the bright stadium/stickman low-poly look, and the "fail" hook has no natural bridge to swipe-to-shoot soccer mechanics without a forced and likely deceptive pivot. Skip unless you can rebuild the concept around a goalkeeper/defender fail moment that ties directly into a shooting gameplay reveal.

Hard pass. The archetype's dark, muted palette (navy/olive/orange) and somber cross-section reveal aesthetic clash directly with Crazy Kick's sunny yellow/sky-blue low-poly stickman world, and a static architectural pan has zero connection to a swipe-to-score football loop. Audience overlap is also weak — Eiffel/bunker curiosity hooks index toward 4X, sim, or puzzle-mystery players, not arcade sports fans. Don't adapt this; if curiosity is a desired emotional pitch, build a sports-native version (e.g., "what's hidden behind this impossible defender wall?").

This is a classic Bus Jam / puzzle "relaxation" hook — its calm, top-down stylus puzzle aesthetic clashes hard with Crazy Kick's energetic, competitive 3D stickman soccer DNA, and the sandy-yellow/sky-blue palette doesn't align with the orange/blue puzzle look. Mechanically there's no transferable signal: swipe-to-aim physics shots and dodging defenders cannot be honestly conveyed by a parking-lot sliding puzzle, so usage would risk misleading-ad policy flags and poor D1 retention. Only minor audience overlap on the broad casual male skew — recommend skipping this archetype unless we test a deliberate fake-puzzle-to-soccer bait-and-switch, which carries store-policy risk.

The "fail" hook concept is broadly transferable in hyper-casual UA, but this specific archetype is anchored to a Bus Jam / parking puzzle context — the visuals (top-down vehicle grid, primary red/green/blue palette) and the tap-to-route mechanic clash hard with Crazy Kick's swipe-based, third-person stickman soccer loop and sunny yellow/sky-blue palette. We'd have to rebuild the creative from scratch (swap to a missed shot or own-goal fail, restyle to pitch/stickman), at which point we're only borrowing the "WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD" overlay pattern. Recommend testing the fail-bait copy formula on a custom soccer-fail shoot, but do not adapt this asset directly.

brief.title: Smart / Genius / Psychopath Trick-Shot Ladder
brief.hook_3s: A stickman striker curves a glowing trajectory trail around three defenders into the top corner — net ripples, crowd flashes, "SMART" stamps the screen.
scene_flow: 0-3s HOOK: Tight POV swipe — finger drags across the screen, lime-green trajectory arcs past one defender into the goal. Overlay: SMART.
3-7s ESCALATE: Same camera, now two defenders sliding in. Swipe curves harder, ball bends mid-air, slots top-bin. Overlay: GENIUS.
7-12s PEAK: Chaotic level — 4 defenders, moving keeper, tiny goal window. Swipe traces an absurd S-curve, ball ricochets off post and in. Overlay: PSYCHOPATH. Level tracker jumps 1 → 7 → 23.
12-15s CTA: Phone-frame mockup of the App Store icon, finger taps install. Overlay: YOUR TURN — CAN YOU SCORE?
brief.visual_direction: Lock to the game's sunny stadium DNA: sandy yellow (#F5D77F) pitch lines and sand textures, sky blue (#1CB4F0) sky and goal frame highlights, bright green (#99CC33) trajectory trails, level-tracker pills, and "GENIUS" stamp accents. Keep the 3D hyper-casual low-poly look — simple stickman striker, blocky defenders, chunky physics ball. Camera is the existing slightly-elevated behind-the-ball angle from the actual game; no cinematic reframes. Each "stamp" overlay snaps in with a quick zoom + chromatic punch but never covers the trajectory arc — the swipe is the star.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Crazy Kick rotation is all generic gameplay-video and "street football" bravado — zero meme-format / satisfaction-laddered creatives. This borrows the trending "Smart/Genius/Psychopath" overlay meme (high SoV velocity in the satisfaction archetype) and grafts it onto authentic trick-shot gameplay, giving UA a fresh thumb-stop angle without reskinning existing assets. Test against the Admob video set as the control — expect lift on IPM from the escalating-difficulty payoff structure.

brief.title: Why Is This Level So Hard? — Soccer Fail Bait
brief.hook_3s: Finger swipes, ball curves wide, hits the post — stickman keeper laughs. "WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?"
scene_flow: 0-3s: Tight over-the-shoulder shot of the sandy-yellow pitch. A finger swipes the ball — trajectory trail arcs, ball clangs the post and bounces out. Big red FAIL stamp.
3-7s: Retry. Finger swipes again, ball weaves past two stickman defenders but the keeper tips it over. Level tracker shows 'LEVEL 4' still locked. Confused emoji overlay.
7-11s: Third attempt — exaggerated wobbly swipe, ball loops backward into own net. Stickman shooter face-palms. Overlay: 'CAN YOU WIN?'
11-15s: Cursor taps a perfect curved swipe, ball rockets top-corner, net ripples, confetti pops in #99CC33 green. CTA slams in: PLAY FREE.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Crazy Kick's sunny, low-poly DNA: sandy-yellow (#F5D77F) pitch with chalk-white lines, sky-blue (#1CB4F0) stadium backdrop, lime-green (#99CC33) UI chrome and confetti. Keep the third-person behind-the-ball camera identical to the live game, with the signature dotted trajectory trail, level progression bar at the top, and simple stickman defenders/keeper. Animation should feel intentionally clumsy on the fail beats (overshoots, wobbly trail) and crisp on the win beat — the contrast sells the "looks easy, actually tricky" bait.
text_overlays: WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current rotation on Crazy Kick is all straight gameplay/glory copy ("Kick your way to glory") with zero fail-bait or curiosity-gap framing — this brief imports the market-winning "WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD / CAN YOU WIN?" hook formula they're missing. UA should test this against the existing Admob video stack as a CTR/IPM lift play; the fail→win arc also doubles as a soft tutorial for the swipe mechanic, which should improve D1 retention on installs from this creative.

brief.title: "Ever Played Soccer That Actually Feels Good?" — ASMR Swipe-to-Score
brief.hook_3s: A finger drags a glowing trajectory line across a sunny pitch — "Ever played a soccer game that actually feels this satisfying?"
scene_flow: 0-3s: Close-up of a thumb swiping on screen; a bright green trajectory trail curves around two stickman defenders. Soft ASMR swipe sound. Overlay question hooks the viewer.
3-7s: Camera follows the ball as it threads the gap, ping-ponging off a defender's shin with a juicy physics bounce. Level tracker ticks 1→2→3 in rapid succession.
7-11s: Montage of 3 satisfying goals — top-corner net ripple, keeper faceplant, ball spinning into slow-mo. Each goal pops a green +1 confetti burst.
11-14s: Level counter zooms to 27, trajectory trails layer into a mesmerizing pattern across the sunny yellow pitch.
14-15s: End card: ball rolls into goal, net says 'CRAZY KICK!' — CTA button pulses.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Crazy Kick's signature sandy-yellow pitch (#F5D77F) and sky-blue stadium backdrop (#1CB4F0), with the bright green (#99CC33) reserved for the trajectory trail, level-up confetti, and CTA — making "satisfaction" visually legible through color rhythm, not stylus-puzzle calm. Keep the 3D hyper-casual low-poly stickman aesthetic intact: chunky defenders, exaggerated ball physics, clean shadowless lighting. The "ASMR satisfaction" feeling comes from slow-mo ball spin, net ripples, and the rhythmic stacking of trajectory trails — never from puzzle-sliding visuals.
text_overlays: Ever played soccer that feels THIS good?
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Crazy Kick rotation leans hard on "insane street football" energy and generic playable/video CTAs — none tap the trending "satisfaction/ASMR question-hook" format that's winning across casual right now. This brief imports that proven hook structure ("Ever played a game that actually feels…?") but honestly delivers Crazy Kick's real swipe-to-aim physics, avoiding the bus-jam bait-and-switch policy risk flagged in game-fit. UA team: test against the Youtube "street football glory" creative to isolate hook-style lift on IPM.
